title: The Lower Stone Spiral

features.name: compression chamber
features.desc-on-touch: stone upon stone, layer after layer pressed down by the weight of the archive above; you feel the pressure in your chest and realize this is how the earth learns patience

features.name: spiral descent marker
features.desc-on-touch: chalk marks on stone, spiraling down into darkness; the notation is old but still legible, though legible of what, you cannot say—it might be a countdown, or a prayer, or a recipe for stone

features.name: the fossil archive node
features.desc-on-touch: a casing sealed in amber-colored stone, the display still faintly glowing; whatever files it held have been compressed into mineral form, unreadable but still broadcasting on frequencies the stone itself remembers

features.name: the waiting well
features.desc-on-touch: a perfect cylindrical shaft that drops beyond sight; water drips at intervals so precise they feel intentional, counting time in a language only stone understands

features.name: strata map wall
features.desc-on-touch: the stone face here shows hundreds of thin layers, each a different grey; you could spend days reading the color gradations, but something tells you the meaning shifts depending on which direction you look

inscription: compression teaches what growth cannot: that waiting, too, is a form of becoming, and that the deepest places are often where the least has changed

file.name: Stone_Stratification_Pores_Fossil_Record.txt
file.note: a stratigraphic column dated at 485 million years, but the corruption has inserted an email timestamp mid-column: 'Leif, the spiral is real. come. —K' dated 2019-09-17

exits.label: back up the spiral toward stone's surface
where the spiral curves toward mineral memory
